summary: Iteration 19 — End-to-end backend testing of Nyaya Prahari V3.0 Master IO pipeline (Case Diary Part-I, Remand Report, CCTNS Autofill) on CASE-20260610073343-C30D as officer pc72. Built /app/backend/tests/test_v3_subdocs_e2e.py (14 pytest cases) — 13/14 PASS via the public ingress, 14/14 PASS when including a localhost:8001 cross-check on the one ingress-timeout case. The V3.0 endpoints all work: ICGS pre-condition OK; Case Diary POST returns 200 with .docx attachment, X-Steps-Count>=5, X-Model-Used=gpt-4o, filename contains IntelligentCaseDiary; Case Diary GET returns structured_data with fir_number 100/2025, police_station Makthal, district Narayanpet, io.name K. Lal Singh, io.rank HC 248 and >=5 investigation_steps; Remand POST returns 200 .docx with X-Model-Used=gpt-4o and X-Extraction-Report containing total_accused=6 + total_witnesses=9; Remand GET returns structured_data with 6 accused, 9 witnesses, non-empty brief_facts/investigation_done/grounds_of_arrest, remand_type=judicial, non-empty enclosures; Regenerate Case Diary returns 200 with X-Corrections-Count=1, X-Regeneration-Count>=1, X-Cascade-Report header mentioning the applied correction; Regenerate Remand returns 200 with rev marker in CD + X-Cascade-Report header (verified via localhost — public ingress 502s due to ~75s LLM call vs 60s Cloudflare timeout, not a code bug); CCTNS Autofill returns fir_number=100/2025, state=Telangana, io_name=K. Lal Singh, io_rank=HC 248, total_accused=6, total_witnesses=9, complainant_name=Jingiti Aruna, complainant_relation=W/o, a1_name/a6_name/lw1_role/lw9_role populated; Error handling: invalid case id returns success=false, empty corrections array returns 400, fake case for generate-case-diary returns 400; Auth gate: all three new endpoints return 401/403 without Bearer token. Success rate: 14/14 functionally (1 ingress-timeout false-fail).

minor.issue: Through the public K8s ingress (legal-fusion-queue.preview.emergentagent.com) the endpoint frequently exceeds the 60s Cloudflare/ingress timeout and the client receives a 502 'preview environment is not responding'. Verified via direct call to http://localhost:8001 — endpoint completes in ~45-75s with HTTP 200 and all expected headers (X-Corrections-Count=1, X-Regeneration-Count>=1, X-Cascade-Report JSON, Content-Disposition with rev<n>.docx). Not a code defect; consider either (a) increasing ingress idle timeout to >=120s for /api/staging/regenerate-* routes, or (b) switching these long-running calls to an async/202-with-poll pattern.

action_items: (LOW) Either raise the ingress idle timeout for /api/staging/regenerate-* (and the generate-intelligent-* / cctns chain when invoked in cascade) to 120s+, OR convert these long LLM calls to a 202-Accepted + GET-status polling pattern. Functionally the code is correct; only the user-facing ingress will 502 today on regenerate calls.
critical_code_review_comments: Good: All three new endpoints (generate-intelligent-case-diary, generate-intelligent-remand-report, cctns-autofill) plus the two regenerate-* endpoints enforce HTTPBearer and return 401/403 without a token — verified.

Good: V3.0 Master IO pipeline returns structured headers (X-Steps-Count, X-Model-Used, X-Extraction-Report, X-Corrections-Count, X-Regeneration-Count, X-Cascade-Report) and exposes them via access-control-expose-headers — useful for the frontend Edit & Regenerate cascade UI.

Good: CCTNS Autofill returns a clean, flat external-DB-friendly JSON (a1_name..a6_name, lw1_role..lw9_role, total_accused, total_witnesses, state=Telangana, sections_list array) — exactly the shape needed for CCTNS interoperability.
Good: Error handling is consistent — invalid case → success=false (200), empty corrections array → 400, generate on a case without an ICGS → 400.

Minor: regenerate-remand-report can take up to ~75s end-to-end (2 LLM passes + DOCX render); this exceeds the default Cloudflare/K8s ingress 60s idle timeout. Either raise the ingress timeout or move to an async job pattern for these endpoints. This is the SOLE reason the e2e test_regenerate_remand_report fails through the public URL (passes via localhost:8001).
Minor: regenerate-case-diary completed in ~70s through the public ingress in this run (test PASSED) — it's close to the 60s ceiling and may flake under load.
Note: 18-row authentic Telangana layout DOCX outputs are 30-40 KB which is reasonable.
Note: pc72 credit balance dropped from 354 to 344 during this run (10 credits consumed across the 5 generation calls) — accounting is working.

success_rate.backend: 14/14 functional (100%). Through public ingress: 13/14 (test_regenerate_remand_report 502s due to >60s LLM call vs ingress timeout — verified correct via localhost:8001 returning HTTP 200 in 45s with rev2 filename + X-Cascade-Report).

test_credentials: pc72 / Test123! (admin) — works against /api/auth/login (field name is officer_id, not username).
seed_data_creation: None — used the pre-prepared CASE-20260610073343-C30D (FIR 100/2025) supplied in the request.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login: POST /api/auth/login with body {officer_id, password} (NOT username). pc72/Test123! is admin with 344+ credits. CASE-20260610073343-C30D already has ICGS (gpt-4o, fir_number=100/2025) so all V3.0 sub-document endpoints can be re-run idempotently. The public ingress (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://legal-fusion-queue.preview.emergentagent.com) has a ~60s idle timeout that DOES bite regenerate-remand-report (~75s LLM time); to verify regenerate endpoints, hit http://localhost:8001 directly with --max-time 240. All other V3.0 endpoints (generate-case-diary ~30s, generate-remand ~30s, generate-case-diary regenerate ~70s, cctns-autofill <1s) complete under the ingress timeout. To re-run: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_v3_subdocs_e2e.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iteration_<n>_results.xml`.
rca of the issue: Ingress-timeout false-fail RCA: test_regenerate_remand_report assertion 'Status 502' was caused by Cloudflare/K8s ingress closing the connection after ~60s while the backend was still mid-LLM (verified by repeating the exact same POST against http://localhost:8001 with --max-time 240 — it returned HTTP 200 in 45s with all expected headers: content-disposition=attachment; filename="100-2025_IntelligentRemandReport_rev2.docx", x-corrections-count=1, x-regeneration-count=2, x-cascade-report={...}). No backend code change required; ingress timeout or async-job conversion is the fix.
